paper_title: Experimental Design and Sample Collection in Eel Pond

methods.description: Collection of surface sediment and site water from Eel Pond for experimental analysis.

steps.description: We used a benthic grab deployed from a small boat to collect surface sediment from five locations within Eel Pond in Woods Hole Massachusetts.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment using benthic grab

steps.description: Surface sediments (1-2 cm) from each grab were sectioned off with a knife, pooled together, and stored on ice until arrival at Princeton University.
steps.searchable_summary: section and pool surface sediments, store on ice

steps.description: Additionally, we collected 120 L of site water from Eel Pond, filtered it through a Whatman® GF/F filter and stored it in the dark until the mesocosms were constructed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and filter site water, store in dark

methods.description: Establishment and maintenance of mesocosms for experimental analysis of sediment and water interactions.

steps.description: Four replicate mesocosms were established, each containing 3 kg of sediments overlain with 20 liters of filtered site water.
steps.searchable_summary: establish mesocosms with sediment and water

steps.description: Sediments from the initial grabs were thoroughly homogenized and visible macrofauna were removed.
The 3 kg of sediment was then distributed evenly over the 0.1 m2 area of the mesocosm to a depth of approximately 2 cm.
20 L of filtered site water was then added to the mesocosm and sediments were allowed to settle for 24 h prior to sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: add water to mesocosm and allow sediment settling

steps.description: Each mesocosm was fitted with a tightly sealed lid containing a two-port valve.
steps.searchable_summary: fit mesocosm with sealed lid and valve

steps.description: One port of the valve was fitted with an air stone to gently circulate air through the overlying waters.
steps.searchable_summary: fit valve with air stone for aeration

steps.description: The other port contained a sampling tube for removal of water for nutrient analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: install sampling tube for water removal

steps.description: The mesocosms were maintained in a darkened room for 45 days.

methods.description: Procedures for sampling and analyzing sediment and water from mesocosms over the experimental period.

steps.description: Initial duplicate samples of sediment from the homogenized pool were collected with a 5 cc syringe corer and stored at −80 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store initial sediment samples

steps.description: Two 15 mL aliquots of initial water were also collected for later nutrient analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: collect initial water samples for nutrient analysis

steps.description: Every day or every other day water was withdrawn for nutrient analysis using a syringe to draw water through the sampling valve.
pH and dissolved oxygen were measured on 20 mL of the withdrawn water using a YSI handheld meter.
Two 15 mL aliquots were also removed and were filtered through a Whatman® GF/F filter and stored frozen for later nutrient analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and store water aliquots for analysis

steps.description: Approximately once per week each mesocosm was opened and a sterile 5 cc syringe corer was used to remove an entire sediment column from the mesocosm.
Sediment cores (3-4 cores taken through the entire depth of the sediment column) were collected, homogenized, and split between duplicate cryovials for immediate storage at −80 • C.
